steps.title: Identify favorable and total outcomes
steps.content: There are $52$ choir singers total, and $13$ of them are altos. The favorable outcomes are the altos.

steps.title: Write the probability
steps.content: Probability is favorable outcomes over total outcomes: $$P(\text{alto})=\frac{13}{52}$$

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A, $\frac{1}{52}$, is one specific choir singer.
  • Choice B, $\frac{13}{52}$, uses the $13$ altos out of $52$ singers. Correct.
  • Choice C, $\frac{39}{52}$, is the probability of not selecting an alto.
  • Choice D, $\frac{52}{52}$, would mean every choir singer is an alto.
That confirms B.
